completedItems: Implemented and committed a dedicated `/chat` SvelteKit route with recognizable agent header/date control, scrollable chronological message history, reply controls, composer, loading/empty/error states, and dev-only seed/clear buttons.
Connected the chat route to Convex via `convex-svelte` using Svelte 5 runes rather than React-style state patterns.
Added anonymous browser session scoping for local chat persistence so message reads/writes are scoped per authenticated user or local session.
Added indexed, bounded Convex message reads and scoped mutations with validators for listing, sending, seeding, clearing, and automated context refresh placeholders.
Added `chatContext.ts` helper for agent-ready context windows, reply context IDs, and grouped consecutive user messages.

Polished responsive styling for desktop and mobile widths with stable full-height layout, smooth scroll behavior, sticky date dividers, keyboard date selection, hover/focus states, reduced-motion handling, and OKLCH/tinted color choices.
Committed changes in git commit `6037dbd` (`feat(chat): build polished Convex chat route`).
unfinishedItems: Manual browser validation of keyboard, mouse click, hover interactions, scroll/date jumps, and reply target behavior was not performed in this worker pass.
Manual Convex wipe/seed workflow validation in a running development Convex environment was not performed in this worker pass.
`npm test -- --run` was not run because the app currently has no `test` script.
Repository still has an unstaged `.DS_Store` modification that was not part of this feature and was intentionally left uncommitted.

commandsExecuted.command: git status --short && find app/src/routes -maxdepth 3 -type f | sort | head -50 && ls .dev/docs

python3 - <<'PY' ... PY
cd app && npm run check

rg "#[0-9a-fA-F]|color: white|background: white|border-left: [2-9]|—|backdrop-filter" app/src/routes/chat/+page.svelte app/src/convex/chat.ts || true
cd app && npm run check
cd app && npm run lint && npm run build
cd app && npx prettier --write src/routes/chat/+page.svelte src/convex/chat.ts && npm run lint && npm run build
git status --short && git diff --stat && git diff -- app/src/routes/chat/+page.svelte | head -120

git add app/src/routes/chat/+page.svelte app/src/convex/chat.ts app/src/convex/schema.ts app/src/convex/chatContext.ts app/src/convex/_generated/api.d.ts && git commit -m "feat(chat): build polished Convex chat route" && git status --short

commandResults.output: Prettier formatted changed files. Lint passed with 4 warnings in generated Convex files about unused eslint-disable directives. Build completed successfully; adapter-auto noted no supported production environment detected.
Created commit 6037dbd feat(chat): build polished Convex chat route.

issuesDiscovered: Impeccable context loader found no PRODUCT.md or DESIGN.md in the project, so project-specific product/design context was unavailable.
ESLint reports existing warnings in generated Convex files (`src/convex/_generated/*`) about unused eslint-disable directives; no lint errors.
SvelteKit build succeeds but adapter-auto warns that no supported production environment was detected, which is expected until an adapter/deploy target is configured.
Manual interaction and Convex environment checks remain for validation because this worker pass did not start the app or a Convex dev deployment.

validationAssertionsTouched.notes: `/chat` exists as `app/src/routes/chat/+page.svelte`, and it contains labeled agent header, conversation history, and message composer regions.
Messages are loaded from Convex, converted with Svelte derived state, grouped by day, and rendered as continuous chronological history without React-style state patterns.
CSS uses min-width guards, clamp sizing, full-height mobile layout, wrapping header actions, bounded message widths, and media/container queries to avoid horizontal overflow.
Convex reads are bounded and indexed, loading skeletons reserve space, and no SvelteKit load function was introduced, so no non-serializable load data was added.
The history panel uses contained overflow, CSS smooth scrolling, date jump scrollIntoView, post-send scrollTo, sticky date dividers, and reduced-motion fallback.
